text: A compound proposition that is always true regardless of individual truth values is called a/an _____.
If a compound proposition is always false regardless of individual truth values, it is a/an _____.
To show two compound propositions are logically equivalent, their biconditional must be a/an _____.
Which truth assignment disproves that \((p \land q) \rightarrow r\) is a tautology?
De Morgan's law states \(\neg (p \lor q) \equiv\) _____.
What is the negation of the statement: "The child is at least 8 years old and at least 57 inches tall" using De Morgan's laws?
answer: The child is less than 8 years old or shorter than 57 inches.
a: The child is less than 8 years old and shorter than 57 inches.
b: The child is less than 8 years old or shorter than 57 inches.
c: The child is at least 8 years old or at least 57 inches tall.
d: The child is at least 8 years old and shorter than 57 inches.

text: How can one prove two compound propositions are not logically equivalent?
answer: Show they have different truth values for some assignment.

answer: It is true regardless of \(p\) and \(q\) values.
a: It is false when \(p\) is false.
b: It is true regardless of \(p\) and \(q\) values.

answer: The child is less than 8 or shorter than 57.
